ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk (notably /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/{lib,src}/ and /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/{lib,src}/). The ONLY Haskell you may read is the SANCTIONED BSD library /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6/. Do NOT read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md, and do NOT read any AUDIT.md anywhere in the cleanroom. No web access. You MAY read .spthy example files under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples/. No git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction in workspace QUERIES.log; record behavioral facts in BEHAVIOR.md with probe provenance; comments describe current behavior only. Oracle scripts carry OOM guards — preserve them; at most 6 concurrent oracle invocations. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary.

Workspace: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/ (read/write freely inside it, except AUDIT.md). Crate: workspace/pretty-clean (R1 term core + signature is done: 41 tests, whole-block parity on the 10 round-1 files). Re-read SPEC.md, your own BEHAVIOR.md and QUERIES.log first. Oracle: oracle/pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy> [flags] prints the exact theory echo to reproduce.

PART 1 — two R1 laws are WRONG (falsified against the wider corpus; fix both, correct BEHAVIOR.md, keep every still-true test):
1a. EQUATION ORDERING: your signature block byte-sorts equations by rendered text. The reference does NOT byte-sort. Two corpus counter-examples: sapic/fast/GJM-contract/contract.spthy — the two equations for checkpcs print in the OPPOSITE of your byte order (visible ~line 11 of its echo); esorics23-bluetooth/models/mesh.spthy — the get_b1/get_b2 equations interleave differently than byte order (~line 23). Run the oracle on both files, study the orders, then pin the true law by probing constructed theories: vary declaration order, interleave equations of different functions, duplicate spellings, builtin-vs-user equations — determine what orders the list (hypotheses to eliminate: input declaration order preserved; grouped per function symbol in first-declaration order; something arity- or name-keyed applied to a non-rendered form). Your law must reproduce BOTH counter-examples and all 10 round-1 files.
1b. WIDE-TUPLE WRAP: when a tuple inside an equations item is too wide for one line, the reference breaks the < and > onto THEIR OWN lines; your pair_doc attaches them to the first/last elements. mesh.spthy's equations block (echo ~lines 33-37, the aes_cmac equation) is the corpus witness. Probe minimal constructed cases (tuple just over the wrap threshold, nested tuples, tuple in lhs vs rhs) to pin the exact layout (what indents, where the elements sit, comma placement), fix the Doc construction, and keep the non-wrapping tuple rendering byte-identical.
After both fixes: assert whole-signature-block parity for the 10 round-1 files PLUS contract.spthy and mesh.spthy (extract their signature blocks as new targets via the oracle).

PART 2 — sub-target R2 per SPEC.md: RULE rendering. 'rule NAME:' with attributes, the premise/action/conclusion form [..] --[..]-> [..] (and the no-action arrow form), fact rendering (persistent !F, linear, fact annotations like [+]/[-] as observed), embedded terms via your R1 term core, the 'variants (modulo AC)' comment block TEXT (the substitutions are opaque pre-computed input — slice them from oracle output), loop-breaker annotations as observed, and macro/let surface if it appears in the echo. Create round2/ materials yourself: pick a curated set of ~12 corpus files spanning rule variety (persistent facts, AC variants, rule attributes, embedded lets, xor/dh/multiset terms), materialize their echoes as targets, and iterate. Acceptance: whole-rule-block byte parity (every rule in each curated file) asserted in tests, plus probe-pinned laws in BEHAVIOR.md for every layout decision (wrap behavior of long fact lists, indentation, blank-line placement between rules).

All cargo tests green, zero warnings, std-only (plus the vendored BSD Doc engine). Do NOT edit anything outside /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/.

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

1	# Clean-room task: theory PRETTY-PRINTER
2	
3	Build a standalone Rust crate `pretty-clean` in `workspace/` that reimplements,
4	from BLACK-BOX BEHAVIOR ONLY, the tamarin-prover binary's **theory echo** — the
5	`theory <name> begin … end` text it prints at theory-load. Given a
6	parsed/closed theory (data model in `interface/ast_types.rs`), produce
7	byte-identical render text and the API in `interface/required_api.md`.
8	
9	## The observable boundary
10	
11	`oracle/pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy>` runs the Haskell binary **WITHOUT
12	`--prove`** and prints the EXTRACTED THEORY ECHO — exactly the bytes you must
13	reproduce. Running without `--prove` is deliberate and load-bearing:
14	
15	* the echo prints at theory-load (~0.25 s/file), so the gate is fast; and
16	* **no proof search runs**, so every lemma renders `by sorry` and NO proof
17	  tree is emitted. The proof/constraint-system renderer is solver output, not
18	  pretty-printer surface, and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE (see "Stays ported").
19	
20	The extracted block is `theory <name> begin … end` MINUS two trailing
21	formal-comment blocks the tool appends inside that span and everything after
22	`end`:
23	
24	* the wellformedness report (`/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */`
25	  or the multi-line `/*\nWARNING …\n*/`) — a **separate slice** owned by the
26	  wellformedness cluster; do not reimplement it here;
27	* the volatile `/*\nGenerated from: …\n*/` build stamp (version / git rev /
28	  compile time); and
29	* the `====… summary of summaries …` after `end` (carries processing time).
30	
31	Interior comments — per-rule `variants (modulo AC)` blocks, `has exactly the
32	trivial AC variant`, and `guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples`
33	— are KEPT: they are your output. The extraction awk is embedded in the oracle
34	and in the acceptance gate identically, so
35	`diff <(pretty_oracle.sh f) <(pretty-clean-render f)` == the gate verdict.
36	
37	## Acceptance gate (full corpus, primary oracle AND integration check)
38	
39	`/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/pretty_gate.sh` runs the binary at `RS_PATH`
40	(point it at your build) no-prove over the whole 419-file corpus, extracts the
41	theory echo, and diffs it against a no-prove Haskell reference cache
42	(`scripts/.hs_pretty_cache`, auto-filled on first run). It reuses the batch
43	gate's `ckey`/`flags`/`strip_env` machinery so per-file canonical flags stay
44	identical. Output TSV: `relpath  MATCH|DIFF|SKIP_*  diffcount`. Warm-cache full
45	corpus ≈ 72 s at JOBS=6. Run it every iteration; `ALLOWLIST` subsets it while
46	you work a single sub-target. **Acceptance is the FULL-corpus gate green, not a
47	fixture subset** (the wf-regression lesson).
48	
49	Measured baseline of the CURRENT ported tree against this gate: **403 MATCH /
50	16 DIFF / 0 SKIP**. All 16 DIFFs are the single `features/auto-sources/spore/*`
51	family and are NOT pretty-printer divergences (see "Stays ported"). The
52	pure-render surface is at 100% today, so your target is: keep every one of the
53	403 green while the ported renderer is deleted underneath you.
54	
55	## Sub-target decomposition (each independently gate-checkable via ALLOWLIST)
56	
57	Ranked by (individual-author yield ÷ isolation) — a good sealed round takes ONE
58	cleanly-observable sub-component at a time. Corpus counts are over the 419-file
59	parity corpus.
60	
61	| # | sub-target | exercised by | ported LOC | isolation | authors it dents |
62	|---|------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|------------------|
63	| **R1** | **term core + signature block** — `Term`→text (operators `^ * ⊕ ++ %+`, pairs `<..>`, `exp`, `diff`, sorts `~ $ # %`, constants `'x'`, `.idx`) AND the `builtins:`/`functions:`/`equations:` block | builtins 319, functions 229, equations 137; `^`251 `*`(mult) `⊕`38 `++`/mset 89 nat 2 | term `crates/tamarin-term/src/pretty.rs` ~410; sig fns in `pretty_theory.rs` (`render_signature`/`render_fun_syms`/`render_equations`) ~130 | HIGHEST — term is a pure leaf (`Term`→`String`, no solver), the foundation every other sub-target reuses; signature is the first block, minimal wrapping | term.rs: meiersi, beschmi, jdreier, PhilipLukertWork, rsasse, charlie-j, BTom-GH, rkunnema |
64	| **R2** | **rule rendering** — facts + premises/actions/conclusions `[..] --[..]-> [..]`, rule attributes, `variants (modulo AC)` text (substitutions supplied), loop-breakers | rules 332 | `render_rule`/`render_rule_body`/`render_ac_variants_block`/`rule_attributes_doc` in `pretty_theory.rs` ~600 | MODERATE — reuses R1 term/fact; variant SUBSTITUTIONS come pre-computed from the solver, you render them | bulk of `pretty_theory.rs` (25-author header) |
65	| **R3** | **restriction / lemma FORMULA rendering** — `∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ ⊤ ⊥ @ < = ⊏ last(..)`, quantifier binder allocation, temporal vars `#i`, the guarded-formula comment | lemmas 384, restrictions 179, exists-trace 296 | `pretty_formula.rs` ~2800 (formula + guarded); lemma/restriction wrappers in `pretty_theory.rs` ~300 | MODERATE — reuses R1 for atom term args; formula layout is its own file | `pretty_formula.rs`: meiersi, beschmi, jdreier, PhilipLukertWork, rkunnema, rsasse, Hong-Thai, BTom-GH, charlie-j, arcz |
66	| **R4** | **macros / predicates blocks** | macros 9, predicates 12 | `render_parsed_macros`/`render_predicate` ~120 | VERY HIGH — tiny, self-contained | mop-up; no unique authors |
67	| — | **proof / heuristic / constraint-system annotations** | (only under `--prove` / web) | `pretty_system.rs` 702, proof fns in `pretty_theory.rs`, `pretty_hpj` proof paths | — | **STAYS PORTED** (solver-entangled — see below) |
68	
69	Recommended order: **R1 → R2 → R3 → R4**. R1 unblocks R2 and R3 (both consume
70	term rendering). Do R1 first: it is the deepest, most-reused, most-isolated
71	core, and it is what makes `pretty_theory.rs` (the 25-author file carrying the
72	pseudonymous web authors) deletable once R2/R3 also land.
73	
74	## Layout engine (NOT part of the erasure surface)
75	
76	Every renderer builds a HughesPJ `Doc` and renders it at width 110 / ribbon 73.
77	That layout algebra is BSD-licensed (Haskell `pretty-1.1.3.6`) and has ALREADY
78	been clean-roomed by the graphdot cluster
79	(`../graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/src/pretty.rs`, from
80	`../graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6`). REUSE it — do not re-derive the
81	fitting logic from the GPL side. Your job is the tamarin-specific part: WHICH
82	combinators, in what nesting, with what literal strings and glyphs.
83	
84	## Solver-entangled inputs — pure render vs. "stays ported"
85	
86	The renderer is a `Theory → text` function ONLY for the surface below. Some
87	displayed data is COMPUTED by the ported closure/solver; the clean crate
88	RENDERS it but is handed it pre-computed, and the computation stays ported.
89	
90	CLEANLY REIMPLEMENTABLE (pure render — R1–R4):
91	* term rendering; signature block; facts; rules; macros/predicates;
92	  formula & restriction & lemma-statement rendering; the guarded-formula
93	  comment TEXT (given the guarded formula); the `variants (modulo AC)` block
94	  TEXT (given the substitutions).
95	
96	STAYS PORTED (solver/closure output, NOT pure `Theory→text`):
97	* **Proof trees / proof methods / constraint-system pane** (`pretty_system.rs`,
98	  the `pp_proof`/`solve_goal_to_doc`/`pp_contradiction` paths in
99	  `pretty_theory.rs`). Only rendered under `--prove` or in the web UI, so they
100	  are OUTSIDE the no-prove echo the gate observes. Leave them ported.
101	* **The DATA feeding R2/R3's comments**: the AC-variant substitutions come from
102	  Maude (`tools/rule_variants`), the guarded-formula negation from the guarded
103	  transform, the closed signature (merged/sorted symbols) from theory closure.
104	  The clean crate consumes these; the transforms stay ported.
105	* **`--auto-sources` injection** (the 16 gate DIFFs): with `--auto-sources` the
106	  closer injects `AUTO_IN_TERM_*`/`AUTO_OUT_TERM_*` action facts and an
107	  `AUTO_typing [sources]` lemma into the CLOSED theory. The RS no-prove path
108	  does not perform this injection, so its echo lacks them vs the HS reference —
109	  a **closure/preprocessing** gap, upstream of the renderer, not a pretty-print
110	  bug. Exclude this family via ALLOWLIST while iterating (see
111	  `round1/exclude_auto_sources.txt`); it is not the pretty slice's to fix.
112	
113	## Method requirements (per PROTOCOL.md)
114	
115	* Derive every glyph, operator precedence, wrap column, spacing and ordering by
116	  experimenting against `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh`; take exact output strings
117	  from observed oracle output (compatibility content, never memory).
118	* Log every oracle query's purpose in `workspace/QUERIES.log` (one line each).
119	* Maintain `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md`: the growing behavioral spec you infer
120	  (operator → glyph, precedence/parenthesization, wrap width, section order).
121	  A deliverable equal in weight to the code.
122	* Tests: per sub-target, a fixture (AST value as Rust constructors) + expected
123	  render snippet, asserted against your impl AND spot-checked against the
124	  oracle. Integration truth is the full-corpus gate.
125	* Dependencies: std only (plus the reused BSD Doc engine). You may NOT parse
126	  `.spthy` yourself; your input is the `ast_types.rs` model.
127

1	# BEHAVIOR.md — inferred behavioral spec of the theory echo (R1: term core + signature block)
2	
3	Every claim traces to a logged oracle probe (QUERIES.log) or a pre-materialized
4	round-1 capture (`round1/targets/*.hs.txt` — itself oracle output). Notation:
5	"target:<file>" = observed in that capture; "probe:<name>" = constructed
6	.spthy run through `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh` (files under
7	workspace/scratchpad/probes/, outputs kept beside them as `.out`).
8	
9	## Layout engine
10	
11	HughesPJ Doc at line width 110, ribbon 73 (SPEC-provided parameters; all wrap
12	observations below are reproduced by the sanctioned BSD engine at these
13	settings). Key engine consequences confirmed against probes:
14	
15	* A one-line candidate is kept iff it fits within ribbon 73 measured from the
16	  current line position; an exactly-73-column line still fits
17	  (probe:e_mid — 73-column `equations:` one-liner kept; probe:b_all —
18	  builtins item that would end at column 75 wraps).
19	* Fill continuations align at the fill's origin column, and that indent does
20	  NOT consume ribbon: continuation content is measured from the alignment
21	  column (probe:b_all functions line 2 reaches display column 79 = 11 + 68;
22	  probe:t_wide App continuation reaches display column 78).
23	* Nested content (e.g. rule bodies at indent 3) likewise measures its ribbon
24	  from the nest, so lines up to 76 display columns appear at nest 3
25	  (probe:t_edge).
26	
27	## Signature section
28	
29	Overall shape (all targets/probes):
30	
31	```
32	// Function signature and definition of the equational theory E
33	<blank>
34	builtins: …        (line ABSENT when no surviving entries)
35	functions: …       (always present — base symbols guarantee items)
36	equations: …       (always present — base equations)
37	```
38	
39	The three declaration lines are adjacent (no blank lines between them).
40	
41	### `builtins:` line
42	
43	* Only these builtins keep a builtins-line entry: `diffie-hellman`,
44	  `bilinear-pairing`, `multiset`, `natural-numbers`, `xor`
45	  (probes:b_<name>). All others (hashing, signing, revealing-signing,
46	  symmetric-encryption, asymmetric-encryption, dest-*, locations-report)
47	  vanish from the line, contributing functions/equations instead
48	  (probes:b_*, b_locrep; target:classic_NSLPK3 has no builtins line).
49	* `bilinear-pairing` INDUCES `diffie-hellman` (probe:b_bilinear-pairing:
50	  echo `builtins: diffie-hellman, bilinear-pairing`; target:sp14_Joux).
51	* Canonical order regardless of source order: diffie-hellman <
52	  bilinear-pairing < multiset < natural-numbers < xor (probe:b_all — source
53	  order was scrambled; target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
54	* Duplicates collapse (probe:b_dupline).
55	* Layout: `text "builtins: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' names)` — items
56	  comma-separated, single space, fill-wrapped, continuation aligned at
57	  column 10 (after `builtins: `), comma attached to the preceding item
58	  (probe:b_all, target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
59	
60	### `functions:` line
61	
62	* Items render `name/arity` + attribute suffix, comma-separated.
63	* Attribute suffix (probe:f_attrs, probe:b_locrep, probes:b_dest-*):
64	  - public constructor → no suffix (`c/3`)
65	  - private constructor → `[private,constructor]`
66	  - public destructor → `[destructor]`
67	  - private destructor → `[private,destructor]`
68	  No space before `[`; no spaces inside.
69	* Sort: ASCII byte order on the name, case-sensitive (`Bb/1, Zz/1, a1/2, aa/1,
70	  cA/0` — probe:f_sort).
71	* Dedup: identical (name,arity) between user decls and builtin expansions, or
72	  repeated user decls, appear once (probe:f_dedup). Conflicting arity or
73	  attribute redeclarations are REJECTED upstream by the tool
74	  (probe:f_dedup2 raw error), so the renderer never sees them.
75	* Typed declarations (`kdf(bitstring):skey`) erase to `kdf/1`
76	  (probe:f_typed).
77	* Base symbols always present: `fst/1, pair/2, snd/1` (probe:b_none);
78	  with `dest-pairing` instead: `fst/1[destructor], pair/2, snd/1[destructor]`
79	  (probe:b_dest-pairing).
80	* Per-builtin function expansions (compatibility content, from probes
81	  b_hashing / b_asymmetric-encryption / b_signing / b_symmetric-encryption /
82	  b_revealing-signing / b_locrep / b_dest-*):
83	  - hashing → `h/1`
84	  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec/2, aenc/2, pk/1`
85	    (dest-asymmetric-encryption: `adec/2[destructor]`)
86	  - signing → `pk/1, sign/2, true/0, verify/3`
87	    (dest-signing: `verify/3[destructor]`)
88	  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec/2, senc/2`
89	    (dest-symmetric-encryption: `sdec/2[destructor]`)
90	  - revealing-signing → `getMessage/1, pk/1, revealSign/2, revealVerify/3,
91	    true/0`
92	  - locations-report → `check_rep/2[destructor], get_rep/1[destructor],
93	    rep/2[private,constructor], report/1`
94	  - diffie-hellman / bilinear-pairing / multiset / natural-numbers / xor →
95	    none (their operators/constants — one, inv, DH_neutral, zero, ++, %+ —
96	    never appear in `functions:`; probes b_diffie-hellman, b_xor, …).
97	* Layout: `text "functions: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' items)` — fill wrap,
98	  continuation aligned at column 11 (probe:b_revealing-signing, probe:b_all,
99	  probe:e_long).
100	
101	### `equations:` block
102	
103	* Header `equations:`, or `equations [convergent]:` when the user block is
104	  declared `[convergent]` (probe:e_conv).
105	* Base equations always present: `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`,
106	  `snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2` (probe:b_none).
107	* Per-builtin equation expansions (probes as above):
108	  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec(aenc(x.1, pk(x.2)), x.2) = x.1`
109	  - signing → `verify(sign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
110	  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec(senc(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`
111	  - revealing-signing → `getMessage(revealSign(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`,
112	    `revealVerify(revealSign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
113	  - locations-report → `check_rep(rep(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`,
114	    `get_rep(rep(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`
115	  - hashing → none
116	* Sort: byte order on the RENDERED equation string; discriminated by
117	  same-head pair `fst(<a, b>) = a` < `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`
118	  (probe:e_adedup) and by every multi-source list (probe:b_all).
119	* Dedup: EXACT duplicates collapse (probe:e_dup); alpha-equivalent but
120	  differently-named equations do NOT (probe:e_adedup keeps user
121	  `fst(<a, b>) = a` alongside the builtin fst equation).
122	* Layout: `sep (header : map (nest 4) (punctuate ',' eqDocs))` — i.e.
123	  all-or-nothing, NOT fill:
124	  - fits on one line (≤ 73) → `equations: e1, e2, e3` (probe:e_mid at
125	    exactly 73);
126	  - otherwise header alone, then EVERY equation on its own line at indent 4
127	    with trailing comma on all but the last — even when several would fit
128	    joined at indent 4 (probe:e_conv: joined length 71 still one-per-line).
129	* Each equation is `sep [lhsDoc, nest (-2) (text "= " <> rhsDoc)]`:
130	  one line `lhs = rhs`; when too long, `= rhs` drops to (equation indent − 2)
131	  (probe:e_long — `  = xlongvariablename1` at column 2 under equations at
132	  indent 4).
133	
134	## Term rendering
135	
136	Observed atoms:
137	
138	| AST shape | rendered | provenance |
139	|---|---|---|
140	| var, untagged/msg | `x` | everywhere |
141	| var, fresh | `~x` | target:cav13 (`x:fresh` source → `~x`) |
142	| var, pub | `$A` | target:cav13 |
143	| var, node | `#i` | targets (lemma text) |
144	| var, nat | `%x` | target:NumberSubtermTests |
145	| var with index k>0 | `name.k` after the sigil (`~x.7`, `x.10`, `XB.10`) | targets: variants + builtin eqs |
146	| pub literal | `'g'`, `'hello_world'` | probe:t_pair |
147	| fresh literal | `~'n'` | probe:t_frlit |
148	| nat literal 1 (`%1`, `1:nat`) | `%1` | probe:t_nat |
149	| nat literal n (`2:nat`) | `%2` | probe:t_num2 |
150	| DH one (`1` in DH theory) | `one` | probe:t_one, probe:t_gone |
151	| DH neutral | `DH_neutral` | probe:t_gone, target:cav13 variant 7 |
152	| xor zero | `zero` (nullary-app form) | probe:t_xor |
153	
154	Composite shapes:
155	
156	* Application: `f(a1, a2)` — `text f <> "(" <> fsep (punctuate ',' args) <>
157	  ")"`; commas attach to the preceding arg, fill space between args, wrap
158	  aligns after the `(`, closing `)` attached to the last arg
159	  (probe:t_wide W2 — continuation line `ylongvariablename2,
160	  zlongvariablename3)`; probe:e_long). Nullary symbols render bare (`shk`,
161	  `zero`, `true`, `f` — targets + probes).
162	* Pair: `<a, b, c>` — `"<" <> fcat (punctuate ", " elems) <> ">"`; elements
163	  carry an attached `", "` (a wrapped line ends with a trailing space —
164	  probe:t_wide W1 byte-checked), fill wrap aligns after `<`, `>` attached to
165	  the last element. Right-nested pairs flatten (`<x, <y, z>>` → `<x, y, z>`);
166	  a pair in NON-last position stays delimited: `<<x, y>, z>` (probe:t_pair).
167	  Source `pair(x, y)` arrives already normalized to a pair (probe:t_pair).
168	* Exponentiation: `a^b`, chains render FLAT for both left- and right-nested
169	  trees: `('g'^~x)^~y` AND `'g'^(~x^~y)` both echo `'g'^~x^~y`
170	  (probe:t_exp2). No parentheses are ever added around exp or its operands
171	  (operand classes that need delimiting — AC ops — are self-parenthesizing).
172	* AC operators mult `*`, xor `⊕` (U+2295), multiset `++`, nat-plus `%+`:
173	  always wrapped in parens, no spaces, arguments flattened across BOTH sides
174	  regardless of source nesting: `(x⊕y⊕z)`, `(a++b++c)`, `(%x%+%y%+%z)`,
175	  `('g'-exponent (~x*~y*~z))` (probes:t_xor, t_uni, t_nat, t_mult2, t_exp2 —
176	  the AC rule variants normalize (g^x)^y to `'g'^(~x*~y)`).
177	  The parens are intrinsic: they appear in every context (function argument
178	  `inv((~x.7*x.11))` — target:cav13 variants; fact argument `Out( (~x⊕~y) )`).
179	  Layout on overflow: `"(" <> fcat (punctuate op elems) <> ")"` — break
180	  between elements with the operator attached to the preceding element and no
181	  fill space (probe:t_uniwide: line ends `…aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa3++`,
182	  continuation aligned after the `(`).
183	* diff: `diff(x, y)` — application form (probe:t_diff, run with --diff).
184	* mult inside exp exponent: `x.10^(x.11*inv(x.12))` (target:cav13) — normal
185	  AC-paren rule, no extra exp parens.
186	
187	## UNOBSERVABLE (recorded per protocol, not guessed as pinned behavior)
188	
189	* PatMatch (`=x` pattern-match marker, sapic): cannot be forced through the
190	  MSR no-prove echo. Code renders `=` + term as a placeholder, flagged to
191	  this entry; must be pinned before any sapic-surface parity is claimed.
192	* SortHint::Suffix(s): the echo only ever shows sigil form (source `x:fresh`
193	  echoes `~x`, target:cav13), so suffix-tagged vars are rendered with the
194	  equivalent sigil.
195	* exp/mult element-level break choice: wide mult probes exceed the
196	  variant-computation budget (probe:t_mult timed out; 4-way
197	  probe:t_xorwide too). Union (probe:t_uniwide) and Xor (probe:t_xorwide3,
198	  3-way with long names: break after `⊕` attached to the preceding element,
199	  continuation after the `(`) pin the fcat construction directly;
200	  Mult/NatPlus/exp-chains use the same construction by structural analogy —
201	  flagged, to be confirmed by the full-corpus gate at integration.
202	* reliable-channel builtin: requires a sapic top-level process (tool refuses
203	  otherwise — probe:b_reliable-channel raw); its signature contribution is
204	  unpinned in R1.
205	* Adapter contract notes: `NatLit`/`Number` carry DIGITS (renderer prefixes
206	  `%`); `NumberOne` is the DH `one`; `NatOne` is `%1`.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Black-box oracle for the THEORY PRETTY-PRINTER clean-room.
#
# Runs the sanctioned Haskell tamarin-prover on a .spthy file WITHOUT --prove
# (the theory echo prints at theory-load; no proof search) and, by default,
# prints the EXTRACTED THEORY-ECHO BLOCK — the exact `theory <name> begin …
# end` text your reimplementation must reproduce byte-for-byte.
#
#   pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy> [extra args]   # -> extracted theory echo
#   RAW=1 pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy> [args]   # -> full HS output (2>&1),
#                                                   #    for parse errors / the
#                                                   #    wf block you are NOT
#                                                   #    reimplementing.
#
# The extracted block DROPS the trailing wellformedness comment (a separate
# slice) and the volatile `/* Generated from: … */` build stamp, and everything
# after `end` (the summary-of-summaries).  Interior comments — rule AC-variant
# blocks and `guarded formula characterizing …` — are KEPT; they are your
# output.  This is identical to the extraction the acceptance gate applies to
# both sides, so `diff <(pretty_oracle.sh f) <(your_binary f | extract)` == the
# gate's verdict.
echo 1000 > /proc/self/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null || true
ulimit -v 25165824 2>/dev/null || true
export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
HS="${HS_PATH:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover}"
f="$1"; shift
DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT="${DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT:-10}"

if [ -n "${RAW:-}" ]; then
    exec timeout "${ORACLE_TIMEOUT:-60}" "$HS" --derivcheck-timeout="$DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT" "$f" "$@" 2>&1
fi

timeout "${ORACLE_TIMEOUT:-60}" "$HS" --derivcheck-timeout="$DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT" "$f" "$@" 2>/dev/null \
| grep -v -e '^Git revision:' -e '^Compiled at:' -e '^[[:space:]]*processing time:' -e '^[[:space:]]*analyzed:' \
| awk '
    /^theory /              { cap=1 }
    !cap                    { next }
    /^\/\* All wellformedness checks were successful\. \*\/$/ { next }
    /^\/\*$/ {
        if ((getline nxt) > 0) {
            if (nxt == "WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!" || nxt == "Generated from:") {
                while ((getline z) > 0) { if (z == "*/") break }
                next
            }
            print; print nxt; next
        }
        print; next
    }
    { print }
    /^end$/                 { cap=0 }
'

1	//! pretty-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover theory
2	//! pretty-printer (the `theory <name> begin … end` echo), derived purely from
3	//! black-box oracle behavior. See ../../SPEC.md for the observable boundary and
4	//! the R1–R4 sub-target decomposition, and workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the
5	//! inferred behavioral spec you build up as you probe the oracle.
6	//!
7	//! SCAFFOLD ONLY. Every render entry point below is an UNIMPLEMENTED stub so
8	//! the crate compiles and the test harness runs (and fails meaningfully) from
9	//! day one. Fill in bodies one sub-target at a time, gating each against
10	//! `scripts/pretty_gate.sh` with an ALLOWLIST (see round1/).
11	//!
12	//! Recommended order: R1 (term + signature) → R2 (rules) → R3 (formula /
13	//! lemma / restriction) → R4 (macros / predicates).
14	
15	pub mod ast;
16	pub mod doc; // BSD HughesPJ Doc engine — reuse graphdot's clean-room port.
17	
18	pub mod formula; // R3
19	pub mod lemma; // R3
20	pub mod macros; // R4
21	pub mod rule; // R2
22	pub mod signature; // R1
23	pub mod term; // R1 (deep core)
24	pub mod theory; // top-level assembly
25	
26	pub use ast::*;
27	
28	/// Whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end`, minus the wf comment and
29	/// the Generated-from stamp (both appended by the caller / stripped by the
30	/// gate). Top-level assembly of the R1–R4 sub-targets. See `theory::render`.
31	pub fn render_theory(thy: &ast::Theory, sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
32	    theory::render(thy, sig)
33	}
34	
35	/// R1: the signature section — header comment, blank line, then the
36	/// `builtins:` / `functions:` / `equations:` declarations. See
37	/// `signature::render`.
38	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
39	    signature::render(sig)
40	}
41	
42	/// R1: one term, rendered at the echo's layout parameters. See `term::render`.
43	pub fn render_term(term: &ast::Term) -> String {
44	    term::render(term)
45	}
46

1	# Required public API (interoperability surface) — pretty-clean
2	
3	The clean crate must expose these entry points so the open side can wire it in
4	behind thin adapters that translate the live `tamarin_parser::ast` /
5	closed-theory values into the crate's own AST subset. Every function returns
6	text that is byte-identical to the oracle's no-prove theory echo for the
7	corresponding fragment. Names are indicative; the boundary that matters is
8	"one entry point per observable sub-target, each independently gate-checkable".
9	
10	```rust
11	// ── (top) whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end` (minus the wf
12	//    comment and the Generated-from stamp, which are appended by callers). ──
13	pub fn render_theory(thy: &Theory, sig: &Signature) -> String;
14	
15	// ── (a) signature block: builtins / functions / equations declarations ──
16	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &Signature) -> String;
17	
18	// ── (b) macros / predicates blocks ──
19	pub fn render_macros(macros: &[Macro]) -> String;        // "macros: …"
20	pub fn render_predicates(preds: &[Predicate]) -> String; // "predicates: …"
21	
22	// ── (c) rule rendering: one multiset-rewrite rule, plus fact & TERM render ──
23	pub fn render_rule(rule: &Rule, variants: Option<&AcVariants>) -> String;
24	pub fn render_fact(fact: &Fact) -> String;               // `Foo( a, b )`
25	pub fn render_term(term: &Term) -> String;               // THE deep core
26	
27	// ── (d) restriction / lemma FORMULA rendering ──
28	pub fn render_restriction(r: &Restriction) -> String;
29	pub fn render_lemma(l: &Lemma, guarded: Option<&Guarded>) -> String;
30	pub fn render_formula(f: &Formula) -> String;            // ∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ @ ⊏ …
31	
32	// Layout primitive (BSD-provenance, NOT part of the GPL-erasure surface):
33	// the HughesPJ `Doc` engine + combinators. Reuse the graphdot cluster's
34	// clean-room engine (workspace/graph-clean/src/pretty.rs, derived from
35	// sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6). Width = 110, ribbon = 73 for the theory echo.
36	```
37	
38	`Theory`, `Term`, `Fact`, `Formula`, `Atom`, `Rule`, `Lemma`, `Restriction`,
39	`Macro`, `Predicate`, `VarSpec`, `SortHint`, `BinOp`: see `ast_types.rs`.
40	
41	`Signature`, `AcVariants`, `Guarded` are pre-computed inputs supplied by the
42	ported closure/solver (SPEC.md "Solver-entangled inputs"); the clean crate
43	only RENDERS them, and their observable text is learned from the oracle.

theory Contract
begin

section{* GM Protocol for Contract signing *}

/*
 * Protocol:    GM contract signing
 * Modeler:     Robert Kunnemann
 * Date:	March 2013
 *
 * Status:  working
 */

/* Some more comments:
 * the security property depends only on the fact that the
 * trusted party never answers two requests for the same
 * contract. It does not so much depend on the actualy
 * crypto used. We could  show that if all participants
 * reach some kind of "End"-state, fairness is given, i.e.
 * either both parties have a signature, or none has.
 *
 * We furthermore added an equation to the signature that
 * allows the communication partner to fake a pcs (but the
 * trusted party can make out that difference).
 */

/* Modifications, Date: Oct 17th, 2017.
 *
 * Bug correction : in WitnessAbort, replaced
 *   in (sign(pcs(sign(ct,sk1), pk(ysk), pk(skT)),skT) );
 * by
 *   in (sign(<<ct,pk(sk1),pk2>,sign(<ct,pk(sk1),pk2>,sk1)>,skT));
 * Otherwise, the security property aborted_and_resolved_exclusive
 * was trivially true, because the event AbortCert(ct)
 * was not accessible. Also added a sanity check for
 * AbortCert(ct), named aborted1_contract_reachable
 * Without correction, tamarin reports 'falsified' for this check.
 *
 * Note: The tamarin spec outputs pcs in Abort1 instead of sign.
 */

builtins: signing

functions: pcs/3, checkpcs/5, convertpcs/2, check_getmsg/2, fakepcs/4

equations:
check_getmsg(sign(xm, xsk), pk(xsk)) = xm,
checkpcs(xc, pk(xsk), ypk, zpk, pcs(sign(xc, xsk), ypk, zpk)) = true(),
convertpcs(zsk, pcs(sign(xc, xsk), ypk, pk(zsk))) = sign(xc, xsk),
/* fakepcs () */

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Materialize the Haskell reference THEORY ECHO for each round-1 target into
# round1/targets/<safe>.hs.txt, using the sanctioned HS oracle only. Run from
# inside the sealed workspace; touches only the HS binary (via pretty_oracle.sh)
# and the corpus .spthy inputs — both permitted materials.
set -u
here=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)
CORPUS="${CORPUS:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples}"
FLAGS_MAP="${FLAGS_MAP:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/file_flags.tsv}"
ORACLE="$here/../oracle/pretty_oracle.sh"
mkdir -p "$here/targets"
n=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r _tags REL; do
  case "$_tags" in \#*|"") continue;; esac
  f="$CORPUS/$REL"
  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING $REL" >&2; continue; }
  fl=$(awk -F'\t' -v r="$REL" '!/^#/ && $1==r {print $2}' "$FLAGS_MAP"); fl=${fl//@cd/}
  safe=$(printf '%s' "$REL" | tr '/' '_')
  # pretty_oracle.sh runs HS no-prove and prints the extracted theory echo.
  # shellcheck disable=SC2086
  "$ORACLE" "$f" $fl > "$here/targets/$safe.hs.txt" && n=$((n+1))
done < "$here/families.tsv"
echo "wrote $n HS reference theory-echo blocks to targets/"

input.new_string:
* Pair: `<a, b, c>` — ONE fill (`fcat`) whose items are `<`, each element
  with its attached `", "` under `nest 1`, and `>`:
  `fcat ('<' : map (nest 1) (punctuate ", " elems) ++ ['>'])`. This single
  construction reproduces every observed shape (probe:p_pw1 wfa–wfd,
  target:mesh k2 equation, probe:t_wide W1):
  - fits → `<a, b>` on one line; a wrapped fill line ends with the trailing
    space of the attached `", "` (probe:t_wide W1 byte-checked);
  - overflow elements continue at (column of `<`) + 1 — the nest 1;
  - when the FIRST element's one-liner does not fit beside `<`, the `<`
    stays ALONE on its line (probe:p_pw1 wfb; mesh k2 inner tuple);
  - `>` sits beside the last element when that element ends a fill line

input.new_string:
## Rule blocks (R2)

Curated byte targets: round2/targets/*.hs.txt (12 corpus files, 72 rule
blocks, every block byte-verified by tests/round2_rules.rs round-trip
parity). A rule BLOCK is:

```
rule (modulo E) Name[attrs]:
   <body>
<blank line>
  // loop breaker(s): [..]        (optional, col 2)
  <variants comment>              (col 2)
```

### Header

* `rule (modulo E) Name:` — the modulo annotation is input data (`E` on the
  closed rule, `AC` on the variant re-render inside the comment).
* Attribute list attaches to the name: `Name[color=#abcdef, no_derivcheck,
  issapicrule, role='r']:` — canonical order color < no_derivcheck <
  issapicrule < role regardless of source order; the LAST color/role
  declaration wins; `process='…'` and external `x-…` attributes are DROPPED
  (probe:p_rattr, target:issue713). Spellings: `color=#<hex>` unquoted
  (lowercased upstream: '#AbCdEf' → `#abcdef`), `role='…'` quoted.
  Fill-wrap aligned after the `[`, `]:`  attached to the last item
  (probe:p_rattr R3, target:issue713 bla).

### Body

* `nest 3 (sep [prem-group, nest (-1) arrow-group, concl-group])` — one line
  `[ … ] --[ … ]-> [ … ]` when it fits, otherwise three rows: groups at
  col 3, arrow at col 2. TRUE sep, not a fill: cav13 Step1 keeps `-->` on
  its own row although it would fit beside the premise row.
* Bracket groups (premises, conclusions, AND the action arrow — one
  construction, probe:p_arr1): `sep [sep [open, fsep facts], close]`:
  - one line `[ f1, f2 ]` when the whole group fits (kept at exactly
    ribbon 73 — probe:p_arr1 PR);
  - else `close` drops to the group's column; `open` keeps ALL facts beside
    it iff they fit that line as a unit (target:mesh DeviceWaitingUser at
    exactly 73 vs ProvisionerWaitingUser one column over);
  - else `open` is alone too, facts fill-wrap at the group's column
    (target:NSLPK3 R_1 three facts per fill line; target:Tutorial Serv_1 —
    `--[` alone although the first action alone would fit beside it).
  Empty list → `[ ]`; empty actions → the literal `-->`.
* Fact: `sep [name<>"(" <+> fsep args, ")"]` — `Name( a, b )`, args
  fill-wrap aligned after `Name( `, `)` drops alone to the fact's column
  when the args are multi-line (probe:t_wide); `Name( )` when nullary
  (target:mesh); `!` prefix on persistent facts (target:NSLPK3).
* Fact annotations attach directly after the `)`: `[+, -, no_precomp]` —
  canonical order + < - < no_precomp regardless of source order
  (probe:p_fann `[no_precomp,+]` → `[+, no_precomp]`;
  target:seqdfsneeded `[no_precomp,-]` → `[-, no_precomp]`).

### Loop breakers

* `// loop breaker: [0]` singular / `// loop breakers: [0,1]` plural,
  0-based premise indices, comma WITHOUT space (probes c_loop, p_lb2).
* E-rule side: on its own line at col 2, between the blank line and the
  variants comment. AC-rule side: INSIDE the comment at col 4, AFTER the
  variants list (probe:p_lbvar).

### The variants comment

* Trivial: `/* has exactly the trivial AC variant */` at col 2.
* Otherwise (col 2 `/*` … `*/`):
  ```
  /*
  rule (modulo AC) Name:
     <body, same layout, nested +2>
    variants (modulo AC)          (col 4; ABSENT when no substitutions —
    <numbered groups>              target:issue777 macro-expanded AC rule)
    // loop breaker: [..]         (optional, col 4)
  */
  ```
* Numbered groups at col 4: the index is RIGHT-ALIGNED to the widest
  `N.` (`1.` cav13/7 variants; ` 9.`/`12.` CH07; `  1.`/`160.` Joux),
  followed by one space; the group's substitution lines align after that
  prefix. Groups are separated by a line of BARE INDENT (four spaces at
  this nesting — trailing whitespace, byte-checked probe:p_var1); no
  separator after the last group.
* One substitution = `lhs $$ nest 6 ("= " <> rhs)` with HughesPJ overlap:
  a lhs narrower than 6 columns is padded to column 6 (`~lv2  = ~lv2.4`,
  `~ltkS = …` at exactly 5+1); a lhs of ≥ 6 columns pushes `= rhs` to its
  own line at column 6 relative to the entry (probe:p_var1
  `~longvariablenameone`, target:Tutorial `request`). The rhs is a full R1
  term with its own wrapping (target:mesh multi-line aes_cmac values).
  The lhs/rhs substitution DATA is solver-provided input; both sides render
  through the R1 term core.

### `macros:` block (rule-adjacent surface)

* `text "macros: " <> sep (punctuate ',' items)` — all-or-nothing: one line
  or EVERY macro on its own line aligned after `macros: ` (probe:p_mac1 —
  m2 gets its own line though it would fit beside m1; target:issue777
  one-line single macro).
* Item: fact-style head with the `)` ATTACHED to the last param line
  (unlike facts), then `) =  body` — two spaces after `=` (the `= ` token
  plus hsep spacing); the body always sits beside and wraps internally
  (probe:p_mac1 wide macro).

### Out of R2 scope (recorded, not implemented here)

* The theory-level `/* looping facts with injective instances: A/1 */`
  comment (probe:c_loop) sits between the signature and the rules — top
  level assembly, not the rule block.
* Embedded rule restrictions (`_restrict`) never surfaced in any probed
  echo (TPM_DAA corpus files lift them to top-level restrictions upstream);
  no rendering law claimed.
* Diff-mode (`--diff`) rule surfaces (left/right variants) are not covered
  by the round-2 curated set; a later round must pin them before diff-file
  parity is claimed.

## UNOBSERVABLE (recorded per protocol, not guessed as pinned behavior)
